title: Phase 1 Demo Map Cross-Reference
html:
Scope: cross-reference the local Paradox grand-strategy map inventory against the
map dataset needed for the Phase 1 Pozhenzi Demo.
Status: reference crosswalk, not imported third-party data.

This file turns the local HOI4, CK3, and Victoria 3 map audit into a practical dataset plan for 破阵子 / Phase 1 Pozhenzi Demo.

The important conclusion is simple:

Paradox data teaches layer separation and tooling shape.
Southern Ming source notes and historical map references provide the actual
historical evidence.
Celestial Mandate stores authored data as a Temporal Place Graph.

The cross-era clean-room quality standard is:

docs/sot/high-level/30-clean-room-world-map-data-standard.md
data/core/map-data/world_map_data_quality_manifest.json

Do not copy raw Paradox or Workshop map records, masks, locators, scripts, or textures into Celestial Mandate authored data without a later license review.

1. Local Paradox Coverage Checked

The current inventory is:

references/map-data/paradox-grand-strategy/inventory.json

It records the second-pass local audit for:

GameLocal source checkedUseful map families foundBest Phase 1 use
Hearts of Iron IVinstall root, DLC, Documents, Workshop pathprovince mask/definition, states, strategic regions, railways, supply nodes, adjacencies, generated distance cachelogistics and region-layer workflow reference; not direct Southern Ming data
Crusader Kings IIIinstall root, Documents descriptors, AGOT Workshop modprovince mask/definition, province history, landed titles, geographical regions, terrain/province properties, adjacencies, generated map objectsidentity/history/region split reference; avoid copying fixed title-tree assumptions
Victoria 3install root, Documents content load, Workshop mods, map-like zip entriesstate regions, history states, strategic regions, province terrain, adjacencies, map-object/city/spline data, generated port locatorseconomy/region/state setup and locator workflow reference; not direct authored geography

Measured highlights:

SourceCounts checked
HOI4 base install13,414 province rows; 1,081 state history files; 304 strategic regions; 251 adjacencies; 909 railways; 727 supply nodes
CK3 base install13,270 province rows; 10,888 province history blocks; 17,462 landed-title blocks; 1,280 region blocks; 353 adjacencies
CK3 AGOT Workshop8,233 province rows; 7,007 province history blocks; 8,904 landed-title blocks; 582 region blocks; 286 adjacencies
Victoria 3 base install783 state-region blocks; 675 history-state blocks; 142 strategic regions; 40,875 province-terrain assignments; 241 adjacencies
Victoria 3 Workshop map mods3018018077 and 3425790174 contain source-like map_data trees and map-object/locator references

Documents-folder check:

  • HOI4 Documents had no source-like map data.
  • CK3 Documents had seven mod descriptors; only local AGOT Workshop resolved to

source-like map data.

  • Victoria 3 Documents had no direct mod map source tree, but content_load.json

pointed to Workshop mods and a generated port-locator file existed.

2. Crosswalk Into Celestial Mandate

Paradox patternCelestial Mandate equivalentPhase 1 data file or runtime layerPhase 1 rule
Province bitmap plus definition tableStable place identity plus optional derived geometry keyed by stable IDscurated/geography/places.json; future generated masks onlyDo not make a province board the source of truth
Province history / history statesTime-sliced local state and historical setupinitial-state/place_state.json, authority_place_links.json, population/economy stateValues are authored from Southern Ming evidence and uncertainty bands
State / strategic / geographical regionRegion aggregate, theater, market region, off-map pressure zonecurated/geography/regions.jsonRegions aggregate decisions and UI, not fixed ownership
Terrain, rivers, heightmaps, province propertiesTerrain/water/flood/wetland/disease/access fields and route costsPlaceState, RouteEdge, RouteStateUse historical evidence; no raw Paradox raster import
Railways, supply nodes, adjacencies, special crossingsRoute edges, route state, flow fields, communication channelscurated/geography/routes.json, initial-state/route_state.json, communication_channels.jsonPhase 1 uses roads, rivers, canals, ferries, off-map pressure; no rail layer yet
Generated map-object or port locatorsDerived frontend locators keyed by stable IDscurated/geography/yutu_map_layouts.json; future generated locator bundlesLocators are projections, not authority state
Map-object textures and presentation assetsStyle pack and renderer tokensdata/core/map-view/map_style_packs.jsonVisual language changes per era; the Place Graph stays stable

3. Current Phase 1 Map Dataset

Current authored Phase 1 map files:

data/era-packages/southern-ming/curated/geography/places.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/curated/geography/routes.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/curated/geography/regions.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/curated/geography/yutu_map_layouts.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/curated/geography/phase1_map_dataset_manifest.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/scenarios/phase1-pozhenzi-demo/initial-state/place_state.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/scenarios/phase1-pozhenzi-demo/initial-state/route_state.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/scenarios/phase1-pozhenzi-demo/initial-state/authority_place_links.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/scenarios/phase1-pozhenzi-demo/initial-state/field_force_state.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/scenarios/phase1-pozhenzi-demo/initial-state/communication_nodes.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/scenarios/phase1-pozhenzi-demo/initial-state/communication_channels.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/scenarios/phase1-pozhenzi-demo/initial-state/intelligence_reports.json
data/era-packages/southern-ming/scenarios/phase1-pozhenzi-demo/initial-state/intelligence_assessments.json
data/core/map-view/map_view_profiles.json
data/core/map-view/map_style_packs.json

Checked current counts:

LayerCurrent count
Place records37
Route records13
Region records5
Yutu layout records1
Yutu node placements20
Yutu route visuals13
Scenario place-state records12
Scenario route-state records13
Authority-place links11
Field-force placements9
Communication nodes10
Communication channels9
Opening intelligence reports2
Opening intelligence assessments2

Reference evidence already present outside Paradox:

Evidence folderCount checkedUse
references/southern-ming/maps/34 fileshistorical map/image references; use for place/route research after source-note review
data/era-packages/southern-ming/source-notes/123 filessource-backed evidence and uncertainty records for curated data

4. Phase 1 Dataset Requirements

The Phase 1 demo needs this minimum map dataset:

1. PlaceIdentity records for every clickable or report-facing place. 2. RouteEdge records for every road, river, canal, ferry, and off-map pressure path used by command, supply, reports, or field-force placement. 3. Region records for court core, route hinge, strategic corridor, military regions, and off-map pressure. 4. Scenario PlaceState records for every place that can open a detailed inspector or affect monthly settlement. 5. Scenario RouteState records for every route edge. 6. AuthorityPlaceLink records for competing court, civil, town-command, disorder, and Qing-pressure reach. 7. Field-force locations keyed to place IDs. 8. Communication channels and intelligence records keyed to the same places/routes. 9. Yutu layout records for frontend placement, label priority, region labels, and route visuals. 10. Map view profile and style pack references for the renderer. 11. Source notes for all historical claims or uncertainty notes for authored approximations.

5. Current Gaps To Track

These are dataset gaps, not broken references:

  • Some yutu-visible nodes are route-only or pressure/gateway nodes without

scenario PlaceState records: place.liuhe, place.xuzhou_gateway, place.luzhou_gateway, place.yangtze_nanjing_reach, place.yangtze_zhenjiang_reach, place.grand_canal_yangzhou_reach, place.grand_canal_huaian_reach, and place.huai_river_crossing.

  • That is acceptable while they remain route visuals, field-force anchors, or

abstract pressure nodes. Add PlaceState before exposing any of them as full player-inspectable places with local economy, unrest, or action panels.

  • Future-seed places should stay dormant until a report, candidate gate, or

player action makes them active.

  • routes.json has route costs and capacities; later flow-field work should

derive explicit AdministrativeDistance, SupplyReach, and GrainTransportReach outputs instead of hand-authoring those outputs.

6. Recommended Use

Use the Paradox material as a checklist:

identity -> state -> region aggregation -> connectivity -> derived locators

For Phase 1, the authored dataset should remain small and source-backed:

county/city/gateway precision where playable;
route-only nodes where they affect flow;
off-map pressure where full-map simulation would distract from the demo;
generated frontend layout over stable IDs.

The machine-readable manifest for this cross-reference is:

data/era-packages/southern-ming/curated/geography/phase1_map_dataset_manifest.json
